This would enable the Management to know the policies and procedures to adopt to strengthen the partnership established with them in the delivery of basic services; discuss with the MAO and find possible solutions to the identified problems enumerated above like: offer competitive prices to the fisherfolks so that they will be encouraged to directly sell their produce to the BBP.  Fresh seafoods sold to the store would likewise mean fresh seafoods offered to the public; and make some arrangements with the Human Resource Office to adjust the office time of the employees manning the BBP.  This would answer the problem on the mismatched Consumers’ “buying period” and BBP’s “selling period”.  The matter shall be further discussed with the Civil Service Commission for consideration; and Strictly adhere to the provisions in the Project Proposal and MOA in operating the BBP to achieve its objectives and become effective.

observations.recommendation:
1.6 We recommended that Management
direct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to evaluate and determine without delay the transactions which
caused the variance and effect reconciliation thereof and to perform the same
regularly, pursuant to Section 181 (c) of the GAAM, Volume I, to present
fairly the cash balances in the financial statements.

2.5 We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant
to exert more effort to substantiate and provide breakdown the beginning
balances labeled as “beginning balance” and “unnamed” in the ledgers
supporting the receivable/payables accounts. As regards the receivables,
request for write-off the accounts in accordance with the provisions of COA
Circular No. 2016-005 if to provide the details/schedules thereof is beyond
possible; submit to the Auditor the schedules and other pertinent documents
for the balances in the ledgers which support the accounts in the FS; instruct
also the Accountant to identify the reconciling items between the balances in
the subsidiary ledger and schedule and to effect such difference to present the
accounts accurately and reliably; and strictly adhere to the provisions of
Sections 4.8, 111.1, 114 and 112 of P.D. No. 1445 so that the accounts in the FS
can be readily verified to its existence, accuracy and reliability.

3.7 We recommended that Management remind the Inventory Committee
Chairman to spearhead the planning and the conduct of physical count of the
Municipality’s supplies and properties and to prepare and submit to the Audit
Team Report on Physical Count thereof pursuant to Sections 119, 120 and 124
of NGAS Manual for LGU and as mandated by the Executive Order of the
Municipal Mayor; instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain Supplies,
PPEs, Biological Assets and other properties Ledger Cards; require the supply
officer and property custodian to maintain Stock Cards and Property Cards,
respectively, per category of the agency’s supplies and properties and to
reconcile balances thereof with that of the accounting records regularly; and
adhere to Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the NGAS Manual, Volume 1 and other
pertinent laws, rules and regulations governing the conduct of physical count
of supplies and properties, maintenance of records and submission of reports
thereof to the Auditor.

4.4 We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant
to submit to COA the JEVs and supporting documents to every financial entry
in the general/subsidiary ledgers of the Prior Period Errors and Other Capital
Adjustments accounts and to prepare and maintain the same to provide
adequate information of the account balances in the FS in compliance to
Sections 111.1 and 112 of P.D. No. 1445.

5.5 We recommended that Management stop utilizing the 30% QRF unless
supported with an SB resolution declaring the LGU under the state of calamity
as required in Item 5.1.3 of COA Circular 2012-002; distribute without delay
the procured goods to the victims of typhoon Vinta in compliance to the intent
and requirement set forth in Section 21 of R.A. No. 10121; refrain from
charging administrative/operating expenses against the LDRRMF. Submit to
COA the legal basis on the payment thereof totaling ₱23,761.28.
Non-compliance hereof may result to suspension or disallowance of the amount
as circumstances may warrant; advise the Accountant to prepare and certify the
monthly and annual reports on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF and the
LDRRMO to submit the same to COA in a manner prescribed in Item
5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and ensure compliance to the provisions
of Sections 2 and 21 of R.A. No. 10121, Item 4.3 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-
CSC JMC No. 2014-1 and Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 in the
utilization and reporting of the LDRRMF.

6.5 We recommended that Management appropriate 1% of the total IRA
for the LCPC fund to have ample amount to be utilized in the implementation of its
PAPs; re-evaluate the LCPC PAPs and ensure that the fund allocated thereof
shall cover its implementation; and direct the MSWDO to reassess its
manpower vis-à-vis the duties and responsibilities and determine whether the
task of overseeing the execution of the LCPC PAPs can be delegated to other
qualified personnel in the office. If not, the MSWDO should adopt measures to
personally ensure that the LCPC fund is optimally used.

7.5 We recommended that Management direct the LSB to include only in
the budget the PAPs authorized under DECS-DBM-DILG J.C. No. 1 s.1998;
advise the Municipal Treasurer to deposit immediately to the account of the
SEF the collections accruing thereto so that this will be immediately available
for the utilization/implementation of SEF PAPs; and adhere to Section 272 of
R.A. No. 7160 and Section 4 of DECS-DBM-DILG J.C. No. 1 s.1998 in the
allocation and utilization of SEF.

8.5 We recommended that Management encourage every barangay or
cluster of barangays to establish an MRF where sorting, segregation,
composting and recycling of biodegradable, compostable and reusable waste
materials will take place; collect non-recyclable materials and special wastes
from the barangays and other sources to be disposed in the designated dump
sites in compliance to Section 10 of the R.A. No. 9003; continuously convene
with representatives from the DILG to fast track the allocated funding for the
implementation of the road concreting project leading to the planned site of
the eco-park and controlled dumpsite; hasten the identification of possible
solution to address the electrical problem which the central MRF is
experiencing for its immediate operation; and ensure compliance to the
provisions of R.A. No. 9003 specially Sections 10, 22, 32 and 33 thereof on the
segregation, collection and disposal of solid waste materials.

9.6 We recommended that Management remind the BAC to evaluate
carefully the qualifications of each participating bidder to the former’s various
procurement activities, inviting attention to the proper disclosure of
relations/interest clause enunciated in Section 47 of R.A. No. 9184. Disqualify
prospective bidders who possess conflicting interest with the BAC and the
Management. Conflict of interest should be interpreted within the ambit of the
definition provided in Section 47.2 of R.A. No. 9184; make a representation
before the Management of MAMEMPUCO to elect its directors and officers
who are not members of the BAC nor officials of the LGU should they wish to
continue its participation in various bidding to be conducted by the
Municipality; and ensure compliance with Sections 47.2 and 47.2(d) of R.A.
No. 9184 and Items 5.2 and 5.5 of GPPB Circular No. 08-2017 in proper
disclosure of relations/interest in the procurement of infrastructures, goods and
consultancy by the LGU.

10.5 We recommended that Management stop providing JOs to agencies
except to the LGU offices and enterprises and to those agencies stationed in the
municipality. Sound fiscal management suggests that funds of the LGU should
be utilized solely for the obligations resulting from the transactions in the
continuous discharge of its mandate. The same principle supports that other
government agencies, bureaus and other instrumentalities have their own
appropriations/funding intended to defray respective expenses towards
achieving of their goals. Inter-government agencies fund transfers/assistance
as may be allowed in exceptional cases and in exigency of the service will be
treated cautiously that government funds must be spent for benefit of the
people; execute a service contract with each of the job orders before they could
render services; Seek SB authority/resolution allowing the Municipal Mayor to
enter into contract with the J0s and to incur obligation in paying their salaries
and/or wages; and adhere to the intent of Section 77 of R.A. No. 7160 and to
the requirement set forth in Section 1, Rule IX of the Revised Omnibus Rules
on Appointments and Other Personnel Actions in the hiring and utilization of
J0s and in pursuit of human resources development.

11.7 We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the DILG
Memorandum Circular Nos. 97-273 and 2007-71 in the conduct of lakbay-aral
or study tours; and submit to the Auditor the above-mentioned documentary
requirements as well as the lacking documents stated in the attached schedule
marked Annex F. Failure to do so may cause the suspension or disallowance of
the transactions.

12.5 We recommended that Management stop paying monetization without
corresponding appropriation. Submit to COA the legal basis/justification of
paying thereof without the required budget in compliance with the
requirement set forth in Section 4(1) of P.D. No. 1445. Failure to do so or after
due evaluation but unsatisfied with the legal basis/justification received, the
Audit Team may cause the issuance of Notice of Suspension or Disallowance
for the total amount paid as circumstances may warrant; and strictly adhere to
Section 4(1) of P.D. No. 1445 and Sections 22 and 23 of the Omnibus Rules on
Leave, Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of Executive
Order No. 292 in the payment of monetization to avoid the occurrence of the
irregularities noted above.

13.5 We recommended that Management stop paying honorarium to the
medical doctor under the consultancy services. Submit to COA the legal
basis/justification of paying thereof in compliance with the requirement set
forth in Items 4.4 and 6, Annex B of the GPCS in the IRR of R.A. No. 9184.
Failure to do so or after due evaluation but unsatisfied with the legal
basis/justification received, the Audit Team may cause the issuance of Notice
of Suspension or Disallowance for the total amount paid as circumstances may
warrant; execute a new contract of services, other than consultancy, which will
fit the nature of work and the qualifications of the hired medical doctor
possessed. She may be hired as job order under the contract of services; and
strictly adhere to Items 4.4 and 6, Annex B of the GPCS in the IRR of R.A. No.
9184 and other related issuances in the payment of honorarium to individual/s
that was/were hired under consultancy and/or other contract services.

14.6 We recommended that Management stop the payments of honoraria to
the doctors without the supporting Contract/Memorandum of Agreement, SB
Resolution and accomplishment reports. Disbursements for honoraria for
services rendered should always be supported with the aforementioned
documents; submit to COA the legal basis of the claims. Failure to do so or
after due evaluation but unsatisfied with the legal basis/justification or the
documents received, the Audit Team may cause the issuance of Notice of
Suspension or Disallowance for the total amount paid as circumstances may
warrant; and strictly adhere to Sections 4(6) and 4(7) of P.D. No. 1445 and other
related issuances in the payments of services.

15.5 We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Accountant
enforce the amount as take home pay not lower than P5,000.00 as stated in
Section 48 of the GAA in order to promote efficient and effective public
service by ensuring that government employees will have enough to support
their basic needs until the next payday.

16.5 We recommended that Management advise the GFPS to complete and
update the information provided in the LGU’s GAD database; direct the GFPS
to always formulate the LGU GPB based on the GAD Agenda or identified
priority gender issues which were derived from the information gathered from
the sources mentioned in Sections 4.1.C.2.1 and 4.1.C.1.4 of the PCW-DILG-
DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01; and make and keep the documents and
records relative to GAD as proof of any matters discussed and approved
during meetings.

17.2 We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should determine the
details of the beginning balances of the unremitted taxes and pay the same to
the BIR without delay.

18.1 We recommended that Management submit to COA the documents
required in the subject Notices of Suspension and to settle the amount stated in
the Notices of Disallowance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant
and Treasurer to effect the required reconciliation and to perform the same
regularly, pursuant to Section 181 (c) of the GAAM, Volume I, to present fairly
the cash balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management remind the Inventory Committee
Chairman to spearhead in planning and conduct of physical count of the
Municipality’s supplies and properties and to prepare and submit to the Audit
Team Report on Physical Count thereof pursuant to Sections 119, 120 and 124
of NGAS Manual for LGU. Furnish a copy of the report to the Supply Officer
and the Property Custodian for record purposes; include the Supply Officer and
the Property Custodian as members of the Inventory Committee. Sound
internal control suggests their inclusion in the committee; instruct the Municipal
Accountant to maintain Supplies, PPEs, Biological Assets and other properties
Ledger Cards; require the property custodian to maintain Property Cards per
category of the agency’s properties and to reconcile balances thereof with that
of the accounting records regularly; adhere to Sections 119, 120 and 124 of the
NGAS Manual, Volume 1 and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations
governing the conduct of physical count of properties, maintenance of records
and submission of reports thereof to the Auditor.

We recommended that Management provide a separate storage room or
appropriate fund to construct a bodega where to place properly all the
unserviceable properties awaiting for disposal and a garage to be utilized as
parking area for the municipal vehicles. Municipal vehicles should be always
parked in the covered garage when not in use; Create a disposal committee who
will spearhead in planning and implementing disposal procedures in accordance
with the existing guidelines of disposing government properties; Dispose all
unserviceable properties per recommendation of the disposal committee for
added income of the LGU; and Strictly observe Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 in
utilizing government scarce resources.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to
identify the reconciling items of the affected reciprocal accounts and effect the
adjustments thereof in the respective books to present fairly the said accounts
in the financial statements.

We recommended that Municipal Accountant record expenses in the
year they are incurred. Unpaid expenditures should be taken up in the books as
payables. Strictly adhere to the provisions of Sections 4(8) and 119 of P.D. 1445
when recognizing financial transactions in the books.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant
effect the adjustments/reconciling items in the respective books to present fairly
the balances in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management immediately call the attention of the
department heads concerned to timely prepare and post to the bulletin boards
allotted for the purpose the required documents/reports enumerated in DILG
Memorandum Circular No. 2011-134 in a manner prescribed in the provisions
thereof.

We recommended that Management prepare and issue a well-defined set
of guidelines/policy governing the implementation of AICS program which must
be aligned with the guidelines issued by the DSWD governing the latter’s grants
of AICS; Determine a specific amount of appropriation for the AICS program
which shall be approved for the purpose; Devolve to the MSWDO the
determination of the grants being the expert on the program; Advise the
MSWDO to keep files of the documents submitted by the applicant. Keeping of
records on AICS program is paramount as one of the standard operating
procedures in government transactions. The practice will serve as a control
against improper disposition of the grants; Set up a revolving fund to expedite
delivery of service. Custody of such fund must be assigned to the Municipal
Treasury; Require the MSWDO to prepare and submit reports of the program
to the LCE for monitoring and evaluation purposes; and Faithfully adhere to
the intent of Section 2 of P.D. No. 1445 in the implementation of any program of
the LGU.

We recommended that the Local School Board plan, consider and
include in the budget only PPAs which are among those authorized under the
provisions of DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1 and within the tenet
enshrined in Section 4(7) of P.D. 1445.

We recommended that Management stop the practice of granting cash
advances for special purposes to persons who are not employees of the
Municipality, not designated as disbursing officer and who are not bonded; stop
the granting of additional cash advances unless the previous ones had been
liquidated; strictly adhere to Sections 89 and 101(2) of P.D. No. 1445 and Items
4.1.4 and 4.1.5 of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant
to prepare and maintain trial balances and financial statements for the 5%
LDRRMF and 20% DF as required in Section 313 of R.A. 7160 and Sections 105
and 110 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that Management execute comprehensible MOA or
guidelines/policies governing the financial subsidies to various schools which
must include among others the basis of the subsidy, the focal objective of the
grants, the responsibilities of the grantor and the grantee upon the disbursement
and receipt of the subsidy, respectively, and the remedies available in case either
of the party is at fault therefrom; Stop the charging of the subsidies to the other
appropriations. Submit to COA the legal basis of appropriating the amount of
P207,415.00 from other budget. Failure to comply may cause this Office to issue
Notice of Suspension or Notice of Disallowance as circumstances may warrant;
Made the grants direct to the school concerned and not to any person or entity not
a party to the MOA. If the recipient school did not maintain official receipts,
the financial aids should be paid to the District or Division where the institution
belongs and which holds official receipts; Ensure that payments of subsidies to
schools must be debited to Due from NGAs to maintain accountability; and
Faithfully adhere to the provisions of Section 2 and 4(1) of P.D. No. 1445 and
Item 3.0 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 in the grant of financial aids to various
individuals and/or institutions.

We recommended that Management appropriate at least 5% of the total
estimated revenue for LDRRMF to be utilized in the reduction of disaster risks
and enhancement of disaster preparedness and response capabilities of the
municipality; Transfer cash to the LDRRMF equivalent to the appropriated
amount to support and accomplish all the annual work and plans of the fund;
Use the fund optimally through the implementation of the PPAs stated in the
CY 2016 Annual Work and Financial Plan of the Municipality; Transfer to a
special trust fund the unspent LDRRMF, except capital outlay, for the purpose
stated in Section 21 of R.A. No. 10121; Disclose in the Notes to FS the
unexpended balance of LDRRMF as prescribed in Item 5.1.16 of COA Circular
No. 2012-002 and as shown in the sample form provided in Annex D thereof;
and Advise the Local DRRM Officer and the Accountant to prepare and submit
monthly reports on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF to COA in a manner
prescribed in Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that Management refrain from realigning/augmenting
savings to any item not included in the approved annual budget; Determine
carefully the savings to be realigned/augmented. Only savings that fall within the
ambit of definition provided in the CY 2016 GAA should be utilized for the
purpose. Such should be disclosed/mentioned in the SB Resolution or any
supporting document as one of the bases for realignment/augmentation; Ensure
that all programs, projects and activities of the LGU shall be reported/included
in the quarterly report on PAPs to be furnished to COA; Submit to COA all
documents as legal bases of the above transactions including the supporting
documents for the purchase of land as mentioned in Section 13.1 of COA
Circular No. 2012-001. Failure to comply may cause this Office to issue a Notice
of Suspension or Notice of Disallowance as circumstances may warrant; and
Adhere to Sections 4(1) and 336, P.D. No. 1445 and R.A. No. 7160 respectively,
in the realignment/augmentation of appropriations.

We recommended that Management formulate and include only GAD
PAPs and expenditures that would support government’s commitment to
promote equality among men and women to resources and opportunities and
which specifically addressed gender issues and concerns; Categorically include
in the establishment of the GFPS the Executive Committee, Technical Working
Group and/or Secretariat. These are responsible in carrying the functions of the
Point System; Set up and maintain the GAD database to serve as basis for
gender-responsive planning, programming and policy formulation; Carefully
plan, identify and consider gender issues and concerns in the LGU that these
may be included in the GPB, with due consideration that optimal utilization of
the appropriated amount for the program will be achieved; Reconcile financial
data included in the GAR with the ones in the SAAOB. The GAD Focal Person
must be mindful that all reports must conform with the source documents; and
Strictly adhere to the provisions set forth in R.A. 9710 and PCW-DILG-DBM-
NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 in the creation and utilization of GAD fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant should determine the
details of the beginning balances of the unremitted taxes and pay the same to the
BIR without delay.

We recommended that Management submit to COA the documents
required in the subject Notices of Suspension and to settle the amount stated in
the Notices of Disallowance.

We recommended and Management agreed to compel and require the Municipal Accountant to maintain and prepare trial balance and financial statements for 5% Calamity Fund and 20% DF as provided for in Sections 105 and 110 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to strictly require the Revenue Collectors to remit/ turn over daily the collections to the Liquidating Officer. Otherwise, she will hold/ not issue additional accountable forms without the full turn-over of collections. Moreover, direct the Municipal Treasurer to immediately deposit the collections of P162,339.98 with the authorized government depository bank and stop the practice of retaining collections in her custody. Further strengthen the internal control in the collection system by strict compliance with Sections 29 to 32 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I. Likewise, direct the accountable officers to file fidelity bond immediately upon expiration of the previous bond issued to comply with Section 101 of P.D. No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The management had implemented instructed the concerned offices to observe the audit finding and recommendations.
observations.reason:
Implemented
Collections were still not turned over to the Liquidating Officer on a daily basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Treasurer to record the financial transactions of the Municipality daily pursuant to Sections 26, 27 and 28 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume II. Likewise, direct her to update and balance all the transactions and reconcile the same with the cash on hand as provided in Section 63 (D) of LTOM. Moreover, direct the Liquidating Officer to maintain Liquidating Officer’s Cashbook for each fund and shall be updated and balanced daily as provided for in Section 63 (B) of LTOM. Besides, compel all the Revenue Collectors to maintain Teller’s/ Collector’s Cashbook to record all collections received and all remittances to the designated Liquidating Officer in accordance with Section 63 (C) of LTOM.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer including the Liquidating Officer did not update and balance daily all the financial transactions in the Cashbook.
observations.reason:
Implemented
The Municipal Treasurer including the Liquidating Officer did not update and balance daily all the financial transactions in the Cashbook.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to stop the practice of splitting the purchases because it is strictly prohibited by law. Base the procurement on Program of Work to avoid splitting of transactions and use appropriate mode of procurement. Besides, conduct competitive public bidding for all procurement which does not fall under Rule XVI of the Revised IRR of RA 9184 pursuant to Section 10 of the same IRR. Otherwise, direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to prepare recommendation for approval of the LCE to use alternative methods of procurement. Further, require the BAC to review the purchase request, request for quotation and purchase orders if completely and properly filled up before award to the lowest bidder is made. Moreover, direct the Municipal Accountant to check and review the completeness of the supporting documents and of the required information therein.
observations.managementAction: The Audit Team did not notice splitting of contracts in CY 2016 but several procurements were not subjected to public bidding even the subject amount was above the threshold provided in R.A. No. 9184.
observations.reason:
Partially implemented
The Audit Team did not notice splitting of contracts in CY 2016 but several procurements were not subjected to public bidding even the subject amount was above threshold.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) daily and forward the same to the Accounting Office together with the disbursement vouchers (DVs) and other supporting documents (SDs). Moreover, require the Municipal Accountant to submit the RCI and Report of Disbursements (ROD) together with the DVs and its SDs within first ten (10) days of the ensuing month in accordance with the provision of COA Circular No. 2009-006. Otherwise, their failure or refusal to submit papers relative to government obligations is a ground for administrative disciplinary action and disallowance of the transactions as provided in Section 39 (1 &3) of PD No. 1445. Besides, failure to render accounts for a period of two months after such accounts should be rendered is penalized in Article 218 of the Revised Penal Code.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer still did not prepare RCI daily and forward the same to the Accounting Office together with the DVs and other supporting documents. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant still did not submit the RCI ROD together with the post-closing trial balance and year-end financial statements in a manner prescribed in Sections 70, 72 and 76 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Section 41 (2) of PD No. 1445.
observations.reason:
Not implemented
The Municipal Treasurer including the Liquidating Officer did not prepare RCI daily and forward the same to the Accounting Office together with the DVs and other supporting documents. Moreover, the Municipal Accountant still did not submit the RCI ROD together with the post-closing trial balance and year-end financial statements in a manner prescribed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to call the attention of the Municipal Accountant on the non-submission of the trial balances and year-end financial statements on or before the fourteenth day of February after the end of the calendar year 2014. Moreover, require him to submit the financial reports together with its complete supporting schedules within the time frame set by law, rules and regulations to avoid sanctions by law.
observations.managementAction: The Accountant still did not submit monthly pre-closing trial balance and quarterly trial balance together with the post-closing trial balance and year-end financial statements in a manner prescribed in Sections 70, 72 and 76 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Volume I and Section 41 (2) of PD No. 1445.
observations.reason:
Not implemented
The Accountant still did not submit the monthly pre-closing trial balance and quarterly trial balance together with the post-closing trial balance and year-end financial statements in a manner prescribed.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the GAD Focal Person to prepare the Annual GPB using the prescribed form and in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, submit it to the Provincial Planning and Development Office then to the DILG Regional Office for review and approval. And develop GAD programs, activities and projects to strengthen the Municipality’s gender-responsive governance and ensure that GAD Fund is utilized in align with the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-01. Moreover, prepare the annual GAD Accomplishment Report (GAD AR) based on the DILG-approved GPB or the enacted GPB following the form prescribed in Annex E.
observations.managementAction: The GAD Focal Person had prepared the GPB and submitted to the DILG for review.
observations.reason: Fully implemented
